questions.question: What is the primary difference between generative AI and other types of AI models?
options: Generative AI works only with labeled datasets
Generative AI is only used for text generation
Generative AI can generate novel outputs rather than just predicting, analyzing or classifying data
Generative AI requires less computational power
questions.explanation: Generative models differ from other types of AI in their ability to generate novel outputs rather than just predicting, analyzing or classifying data.

questions.question: How many key capabilities of generative AI are discussed that will drive future applications?
questions.explanation: Generative AI provides 6 key capabilities: facilitating creative process, suggesting contextually relevant solutions, personalizing user experience, minimizing delay in resolving queries, acting as interface to complex systems, and scaling content generation.

questions.question: What technique do generative models use to produce new content that never existed in training data?
options: Embedding knowledge into latent mathematical spaces and using interpolation
Simple copying and pasting from training examples
questions.explanation: Models embed knowledge repositories into latent mathematical spaces and leverage interpolation techniques to navigate this space to produce new content.

questions.question: Why is producing original content considered cognitively demanding?
options: It can only be done by trained professionals
It takes a very long time to type
It requires complex, non-linear thinking and emotional intelligence
questions.explanation: Creativity involves complex, non-linear thinking and emotional intelligence that makes it challenging to replicate or automate with scripting and algorithms.

questions.question: What challenge during the creative process can GenAI help users overcome?
options: Writer's block and difficulties with visualizing concepts
questions.explanation: Users can overcome writer's block, difficulties with imagining and visualizing scenes, navigating ideas, creating narratives, and constructing arguments.

questions.question: Which GenAI tool is mentioned for visualizing challenging concepts?
questions.explanation: Midjourney is mentioned as a GenAI tool that can help visualize and communicate challenging concepts from text descriptions.

questions.question: What has caused significant decrease in traffic to Q&A programming websites?
options: Introduction of ChatGPT and GitHub Copilot
questions.explanation: There has been a significant decrease in traffic to Q&A websites since the introduction of ChatGPT and GitHub Copilot.

questions.question: Why is finding solutions through search engines often inefficient for developers?
options: Developers must hope someone encountered the exact same problem in the same context
Search engines are too slow to respond
Programming content is not indexed well
questions.explanation: Developers must hope to find someone who has encountered the exact same problem in the same context with a solution provided.

questions.question: What does context do to potential solutions of a problem?
options: Eliminates the need for any solution
Increases the number of possible solutions
questions.explanation: Context narrows down the potential solutions to a problem. A problem cannot be defined without a thorough description of circumstances.

questions.question: What did providing personalization previously require?
options: Complex onboarding forms, excessive data collection, analysis, and segmentation
questions.explanation: Previously, providing personalization required complex onboarding forms, excessive collection of user data, data analysis, and user segmentation.

questions.question: How can Large Language Models act as customer service agents?
options: By transferring all calls to human agents
By reading from fixed scripts only
By asking questions to map customer preferences to product catalogs
questions.explanation: LLMs can ask relevant questions until they map customer preferences and unique needs to a product catalog for generating recommendations.

questions.question: In which domain can GenAI construct dynamic environments based on user interaction?
questions.explanation: In gaming and Virtual Reality, GenAI can construct dynamic environments and worlds based on user's interaction with the application.

questions.question: What type of games benefit from GenAI producing narratives on the fly?
questions.explanation: In Role-Playing Games (RPGs), you can produce narratives and character stories on the fly based on user's decisions and dialog choices.

questions.question: What is the role of autonomous bots in customer service?
options: To collect data for marketing only
To be the first point of contact before escalation to human agents
To permanently replace all human agents
questions.explanation: Autonomous bots will be the first point of contact for customers who want their queries swiftly answered before cases are escalated.

questions.question: Why might customers prefer talking to a bot first?
options: Bots can make exceptions to policies
To avoid long queues and achieve quick resolution
Bots have more control than humans
questions.explanation: As a customer, you may prefer talking to a bot first if it means avoiding long queues and achieving a quick resolution.

questions.question: Who benefits from GenAI acting as an interface to complex systems?
options: Non-developers who need access without technical skills
questions.explanation: Non-developers may need to access information or perform tasks without having the necessary skills to execute commands on complex systems.

questions.question: What can an investment manager ask a GenAI bot to do?
options: Aggregate portfolio performance from company database
Hire new employees for the team
questions.explanation: An investment manager can ask a GenAI bot to aggregate the performance of the portfolio in the company's database.

questions.question: What happens to time required to produce a quality blog post with GenAI help?
questions.explanation: The time it takes to produce a quality blog post is slashed from days to hours with GenAI assistance.

questions.question: What low-level cognitive tasks can GenAI help writers avoid?
questions.explanation: You no longer have to perform low-level cognitive tasks of summarizing research or rewording sentences yourself.

questions.question: What should writers focus on when using GenAI for content creation?
questions.explanation: You can focus on the outline, flow and structure of the content then use GenAI to fill in the gaps, polish and refine.

questions.question: Which is NOT mentioned as a challenge preventing GenAI adoption?
questions.explanation: Challenges include inaccuracy, relevance, quality, consistency, data privacy, cyber-security, and integration - but not slow internet.

questions.question: What do companies want to ensure in model responses for customer-facing applications?
questions.explanation: Companies want consistency and relevance in the model's responses and to ensure outputs are not offensive or inappropriate.

questions.question: Why can generative model outputs be generic and repetitive?
options: They process too much training data
They follow common patterns during generation
questions.explanation: They follow common patterns during generation, which can be generic, repetitive and boring without fine-tuning.

questions.question: What can solve challenges like data privacy and security issues?
questions.explanation: Some challenges such as data privacy and security issues can be solved with software engineering best practices.

questions.question: What is prompt engineering?
options: Writing prompts that effectively program the model for certain outputs
questions.explanation: Prompt engineering is writing prompts that effectively 'code' or 'program' the model to produce outputs in a certain way.

questions.question: What does fine-tuning involve?
options: Re-training the model on your own data
questions.explanation: Fine-tuning involves re-training the model on your own data to improve relevance, quality, coherence and consistency.

questions.question: What can autonomous models do to help with adoption?
options: Take actions on user's behalf for gathering information
questions.explanation: Autonomous models can take actions on the user's behalf on gathering additional information and performing actions.

questions.question: What happens when you prompt a model with only a few keywords?
options: The model returns an error message
You may get a response that may not satisfy your intent
questions.explanation: When you prompt a model with only a few keywords, you will get a response that may or may not satisfy your intent.

questions.question: How does the number of keywords affect search engine results?
options: Fewer keywords make it harder to infer exact intent
More keywords always give worse results
questions.explanation: The fewer keywords you provide, the harder it is for the search engine to infer what you exactly want.

questions.question: Why do generic prompts often result in generic outputs?
options: Generic responses best satisfy generic queries
questions.explanation: A generic response would satisfy generic or low keyword prompts the most, which is why you often get generic output.

questions.question: What is crucial for obtaining relevant and high quality results?
questions.explanation: Context-rich prompts are crucial to obtaining relevant, specific and high quality results from generative models.

questions.question: What can prompting techniques allow models to do with databases?
questions.explanation: Prompting techniques can allow you to translate between natural language and SQL, so the model can talk to databases.

questions.question: What is a limitation of text-to-SQL techniques?
options: They work with all database types
They mainly work with relational databases only
They are too fast to control
questions.explanation: Text-to-SQL techniques are still specialized to mostly work with relational databases and not external systems and APIs.

questions.question: Why might text-to-SQL not be suitable for production use?
options: It requires too much data storage
Probabilistic nature reduces accuracy and reliability
It is too expensive to run
questions.explanation: Their probabilistic nature reduces the accuracy and reliability of results, making them unsuitable for production.

questions.question: What is a reliable solution for providing context from databases and APIs?
options: Wrapping the model with a web server
questions.explanation: A reliable solution is to wrap the model with a web server that obtains explicit instructions via HTTP requests.

questions.question: What advantage does a web server wrapper provide over direct connections?
options: More control and ability to moderate based on permissions
It requires no code to set up
questions.explanation: It provides more control, determinism, safeguards from malicious code, and ability to moderate data based on permission levels.

questions.question: Which Python web framework matches Go/Node.js performance while keeping Python's AI ecosystem?
questions.explanation: FastAPI can match the performance of Go or Node.js web frameworks while holding onto Python's deep learning ecosystem.

questions.question: What type of framework is Django?
questions.explanation: Django is a full-stack framework that comes with batteries included, mature with a large community.

questions.question: What type of framework is Flask?
options: Micro web framework that is lightweight and extensible
questions.explanation: Flask is a micro web framework that is lightweight and extensible.

questions.question: As of writing, what is FastAPI's ranking among Python web frameworks on GitHub?
questions.explanation: As of writing, FastAPI is the second most popular Python web framework on GitHub.

questions.question: Which framework currently leads in number of downloads?
questions.explanation: Flask is leading the number of downloads due to its reputation, community support and extensibility.

questions.question: What does FastAPI provide out of the box that Flask lacks?
options: Data validation, type safety, automatic documentation, built-in web server
questions.explanation: FastAPI provides data validation, type safety, automatic documentation, and a built-in web server out of the box.

questions.question: In which area does Django fall short compared to FastAPI?
questions.explanation: Django falls short in performance compared to FastAPI.

questions.question: What is the recommended capstone project for learning FastAPI with AI integration?
questions.explanation: The capstone project is a job application assessment bot capable of assessing candidates for technical positions.

questions.question: Which models will the capstone project integrate with?
options: Large language model and Stable Diffusion model
questions.explanation: The service will be integrated with multiple models including a large language model and a Stable Diffusion model.

questions.question: What technique will the capstone project use to read uploaded documents?
questions.explanation: The project will utilize Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) technique to read uploaded application documents.

questions.question: What frontend library is mentioned for the UI code?
questions.explanation: The UI code is built on React, a popular frontend UI library.

questions.question: What can the service generate in real-time?
questions.explanation: The service will generate real-time responses to user queries as text, audio or image.

questions.question: What protection features will the capstone project provide?
options: Protections against misuse and permission-based restrictions
questions.explanation: The project will restrict responses based on user permission and provide protections against misuse and abuse.

questions.question: What will the service produce for human-in-loop reviews?
options: Monitoring action logs and conversation histories
questions.explanation: The service will produce monitoring action logs and conversation histories for human-in-loop reviews.
